title: hieracium pichleri subsp. pichleri
intro: Hieracium pichleri subsp. pichleri, also known as Hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the European Alps. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Hawkweed is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
